text:
--- Page 3 ---
A WORD FROM THE EXPERTS
To add flavor and diversify your meals, many condiments such as fresh herbs (mint, oregano, coriander, rosemary, sage, thyme), spices (salt, pepper, paprika, cinnamon, curcumin, cumin), fresh onions, garlic, olive oil and rapeseed oil, ginger, non-industrial honey and sugar can be used!
Everything that is not on the list MUST BE EXCLUDED, in particular fatty, sugary and/or processed products, frozen or canned.
From phase 2, baking soda, baking powder, sesame paste and a few walnuts or almonds (6 to 8 per day) are also allowed.
Finally, feel free to vary cooking methods; they are all allowed EXCEPT frying (frying with olive oil and air fryer cooking are allowed)!
Your dietitian and gastroenterologist are here to support you!
4
Tuna in olive oil or rapeseed oil
Pineapple
All vegetables
Lean red meat
Mango
Sweet potato
Orange
Thin slice of artisanal whole wheat bread made with baking powder
EXCEPT kale, leeks, celery, asparagus, artichokes
Lentils, peas, chickpeas, dried beans
Quinoa
